title: Processes, Signals, Jobs, and Resource Control · Mastery Check
summary: Diagnose a worker that appears hung while preserving evidence, avoiding collateral termination, and distinguishing shell jobs from system processes. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.
Use ps, pgrep, pidof, top-like evidence, and proc data with filters that identify the intended process uniquely.
Send signals from least disruptive to forceful while accounting for handlers, permissions, descendants, and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Process Model · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2207; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.

Which action demonstrates Process Model without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.

content.text: Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2207; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.

content.title: Evidence inspection
content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Process Model result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: The Open Group Base Specifications Issue 8 — Controls portable shell, utility, process, file, environment, and system-interface semantics.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A program file and a running process are the same object.

Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Priority Limits: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2204; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Interpret nice values, scheduling priority, resource limits, and pressure evidence without equating priority with guaranteed resources.
